methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Firehawk Aerospace is a Series A defense and security company that has raised $25M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peers: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $25M total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A-to-next-stage progression, implies a valuation of $50M. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same-stage comparables—Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M)—yields a median valuation of $300M, with a range spanning $150M to $500M.

The two methods diverge substantially, producing a triangulated range of $42M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $175M. This wide dispersion reflects the known sensitivity of early-stage defense valuations to customer pipeline maturity, technical risk, and regulatory pathway clarity—factors not quantified in available data. The stage-progression method may underestimate given Firehawk's sector tailwinds; the peer-median approach may overweight outlier valuations if comparables differ materially in revenue traction or IP defensibility. Further diligence on revenue run-rate and contract visibility would be essential to narrow this range.
